task.title: Configuring virtual machines to use VXLAN

li.p: You configured the vxlan10-bridge virtual network in libvirtd.

context.p: To configure a VM to use a bridge device with an attached virtual extensible LAN (VXLAN) on the host, create a new VM that uses the vxlan10-bridge virtual network or update the settings of existing VMs to use this network.
Perform this procedure on the RHEL hosts.

step.cmd: To create a new VM and configure it to use the vxlan10-bridge network, pass the --network network:vxlan10-bridge option to the virt-install command when you create the VM:
To change the network settings of an existing VM:

li.p: Connect the VM’s network interface to the vxlan10-bridge virtual network:
Shut down the VM, and start it again:
li.codeblock:
# virsh shutdown VM_name
# virsh start VM_name

li.p: Display the virtual network interfaces of the VM on the host:
li.codeblock:
# virsh domiflist VM_name
Interface Type Source Model MAC ------------------------------------------------------------------- vnet1 bridge vxlan10-bridge virtio 52:54:00:c5:98:1c

li.p: Display the interfaces attached to the vxlan10-bridge bridge:
li.codeblock: # ip link show master vxlan10-bridge 18: vxlan10: <BROADCAST,MULTICAST,UP,LOWER_UP> mtu 1500 qdisc noqueue master br0 state UNKNOWN mode DEFAULT group default qlen 1000 link/ether 2a:53:bd:d5:b3:0a brd ff:ff:ff:ff:ff:ff 19: vnet1: <BROADCAST,MULTICAST,UP,LOWER_UP> mtu 1500 qdisc noqueue master br0 state UNKNOWN mode DEFAULT group default qlen 1000 link/ether 52:54:00:c5:98:1c brd ff:ff:ff:ff:ff:ff
li.p: Note that the libvirtd service dynamically updates the bridge’s configuration. When you start a VM which uses the vxlan10-bridge network, the corresponding vnet* device on the host appears as a port of the bridge.
Use address resolution protocol (ARP) requests to verify whether VMs are in the same VXLAN:
Start two or more VMs in the same VXLAN.
Send an ARP request from one VM to the other one:
li.codeblock:
# arping -c 1 192.0.2.2
ARPING 192.0.2.2 from 192.0.2.1 enp1s0 Unicast reply from 192.0.2.2 [52:54:00:c5:98:1c] 1.450ms Sent 1 probe(s) (0 broadcast(s)) Received 1 response(s) (0 request(s), 0 broadcast(s))
li.p: If the command shows a reply, the VM is in the same layer-2 domain and, in this case in the same VXLAN.
Install the iputils package to use the arping utility.
